title: Integrated vacuum for motor vehicle
abstract: An on-board central vacuum system (10) for a vehicle is provided. The on-board central vacuum system includes a vacuum motor assembly (100), a vacuum hose (110) fluidly connected to a vacuum nozzle handle (112) and interior trim (60). The interior trim has an armrest portion (120) and includes a handle pocket (130) that defines an opening (132). The handle pocket defines a cavity (136) that projects below an outermost surface of the interior trim and nestingly accepts the vacuum nozzle handle in a recessed and stowed position relative to the outermost surface.
claims: Claims CLAI MS  What is claimed is: 1 . An on-board central vacuum system for a vehicle, the on-board central vacuum system comprising: a vacuum motor assembly; a vacuum hose fluidly connected to a vacuum nozzle handle; and interior trim having an armrest portion that includes a handle pocket that defines an opening, the handle pocket defining a cavity that projects below an outermost surface of the interior trim and nestingly accepts the vacuum nozzle handle in a recessed and stowed position relative to the outermost surface. 2. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 1 , wherein the interior trim comprises an interior quarter side panel assembly. 3. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 2, wherein the interior quarter side panel assembly is configured into a third row trim of the vehicle and wherein the handle pocket is located between a backrest portion of a third row of seats and a second row of seats of the vehicle. 4. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 2, wherein the handle pocket defines a hose passage provided under the interior quarter side panel assembly, wherein the vacuum hose slidably advances through the hose passage when the vacuum nozzle handle moves from the recessed and stowed position to a use position. 5. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 1 , further comprising a series of stand-offs that are positioned in the handle pocket and extend into the cavity, the stand-offs configured to offset the vacuum handle nozzle handle from the pocket wall of the handle pocket in the recessed and stowed position. 6. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 1 , further comprising a storage cavity adjacent to the handle pocket, the storage cavity housing vacuum accessories therein. 7. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 6, wherein the storage cavity stores the accessories below the outermost surface of the interior trim. 8. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 7, further comprising a sliding door that slidaby rotates along a track between an open position and a closed position. 9. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 8, wherein the door is corrugated. 10. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 1 , wherein the opening of the handle pocket extends longitudinally between a first width portion and a second width portion, wherein the first width portion is wider than the second width portion and wherein the vacuum nozzle handle is positioned at the first width portion in the recessed and stowed position. 1 1. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 10, wherein the handle pocket includes a tang that extends into the cavity, the tang configured to engage and secure to a detent formed on the vacuum nozzle handle in the recessed and stowed position. 12. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 10, further comprising a low power lamp positioned in the cavity. 13. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 12, further comprising a power button positioned in the cavity. 14. An on-board central vacuum system for a vehicle, the on-board central vacuum system comprising: a vacuum hose fluidly connected to a vacuum nozzle handle; and interior trim having a handle pocket that defines an opening, the handle pocket defining a cavity that projects below an outermost surface of the interior trim and receives the vacuum nozzle handle in a recessed and stowed position relative to the outermost surface; a storage cavity arranged proximate to the handle pocket, the storage cavity defining a bin portion for receiving accessories; and a sliding door that slidably negotiates along a track between an open position and a closed position. 15. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 14, wherein the opening of the handle pocket extends longitudinally between a first width portion and a second width portion, wherein the first width portion is wider than the second width portion and wherein the vacuum nozzle handle is positioned at the first width portion in the recessed and stowed position. 16. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 15, wherein the handle pocket includes a tang that extends into the cavity, the tang configured to engage and secure to a detent formed on the vacuum nozzle handle in the stowed position. 17. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 16, further comprising a low power lamp and a power button positioned in the cavity. 18. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 14, wherein the interior quarter side panel assembly is configured into a third row trim of the vehicle. 19. The on-board central vacuum system of claim 14, wherein the handle pocket defines a hose passage provided under the interior quarter side panel assembly, wherein the hose slidably advances through the hose passage when the vacuum nozzle handle moves from the recessed and stowed position to a use position.
